nodes.label: AI for repetitive tasks vs. core skills
nodes.label_negative: AI for core skills like coding and architecture
nodes.grounding_span: repetitive tasks, such as updating pom files for Maven or updating libraries using npm, just take the longest amount of time and then that takes away time from actually implementing new features

nodes.label: AI tool quality and reliability
nodes.label_negative: Low-quality AI tools that break code
nodes.grounding_span: the AI tools we had (especially when they were plug-ins for intelliJ), were very low quality and broke code in 99% of cases

nodes.label: AI doing too much vs. doing too little
nodes.label_negative: AI doing exactly the right amount
nodes.grounding_span: the AI changed a file that was not supposed to be included in the upgrade ... it actually did too much and too little simultaneously

nodes.label: Learning to use AI effectively
nodes.label_negative: Struggling with AI due to lack of guidance
nodes.grounding_span: the more I learn about how to actually use the AI ... then the less it does things wrong

nodes.label: Willingness to adopt new technology
nodes.grounding_span: the willing/unwillingness of different employees toward AI is the exact same that has happened in the past with new technology

nodes.label: Productivity and time efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: frees up my time for finishing JIRAs which the business actually want my team to finish

nodes.label: Professional competence and skill preservation
nodes.grounding_span: coding, architecture, problem-solving, etc. are the real skills I need in my job

nodes.label: Collaboration and effective communication
nodes.grounding_span: communicating with the clients about what tech will be implemented

nodes.label: Satisfaction with high-quality AI tool
nodes.grounding_span: when we changed from a low quality tool ... to a high quality tool ... that's when I felt so satisfied

nodes.label: Frustration with AI overstepping scope
nodes.grounding_span: the AI changed a file that was not supposed to be included in the upgrade ... it broke the tests

nodes.label: Optimistic excitement about future AI collaboration
Caution against over-reliance on AI
nodes.grounding_span: not to become over-reliant on it and lose the skills I need to keep

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through feedback
nodes.grounding_span: only if it does it wrongly, do I provide more follow-up info

nodes.label: Pragmatic delegation with selective oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I'll ask the AI to do something ... and then, only if it does it wrongly, do I provide more follow-up info
